sourceMovie.title: Thirst
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Thirst works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Exorcism of Emily Rose
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Thirst clicked for you, The Exorcism of Emily Rose (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Thirst?
faq.answer: Try The Exorcism of Emily Rose, Possum, Stigmata—each shares DNA with Thirst while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Thirst?
faq.answer: Thirst (2009) is best described as Drama, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Thirst worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
